reason: Critical requirements are not yet met: correct dataset files aren’t guaranteed to be read, star ratings are still heuristic-based instead of LLM-reasoned, and the LLM integration has configuration/robustness gaps (invalid default model, missing temperature, silent fallback). Fixing these will substantially improve compliance, quality, and stability. We are at iteration 0 of 3, so there is ample room for impactful improvements.

specific_areas: Data I/O: Load exactly the required files (amazon_train_sample.json, amazon_test_sample.json, user_sample.json, item_sample.json, review_sample.json) and use them in indexers/profilers.
LLM-based Star Rater: Replace heuristic sentiment mapping with an LLM call that outputs a JSON {"stars": 1-5, "justification": "..."}. Enforce this path in strict mode.
LLM configuration: Source model from OPENAI_MODEL (default gpt-4.1-mini), pass temperature to Responses API, and add robust output extraction.
Strict LLM requirement: Remove silent fallback; raise unless OFFLINE_MODE=1 is explicitly set.
Priors and calibration: Fix item quality_prior smoothing (blend with global mean) and center user/item biases around global_mean in StarRater and related modules.
Prompting improvements: Add few-shot examples from user/item histories to ReviewAuthor prompts.
QA/policy: Add profanity/style checks and aspect alias/lemmatization to improve coverage and enforce policy.
Efficiency: Add a small generation cache keyed by (user_id, item_id, plan signature) to avoid redundant LLM calls during tuning.
Metrics: Prepare evaluation hooks for MAE/RMSE on stars and a semantic similarity metric (if available) while keeping ODD-mode compatibility.

task_understanding.adjustments: Prioritize the hard requirements: use the specified data files and ensure star ratings are determined by the LLM, not heuristics. Treat OFFLINE_MODE as an explicit opt-in exception.

data_analysis.adjustments: Implement a DataIndexer that loads amazon_train_sample.json (train), amazon_test_sample.json (test), user_sample.json (user personas), item_sample.json (item metadata), and review_sample.json (historical texts). Compute global_mean from training data and expose user/item priors and histories.

model_planning.adjustments: Design an LLM star-rating prompt that conditions on persona, item context, and the generated review, returning a deterministic JSON rating. Plan few-shot guidance using 1–2 user/item history snippets. Define strict error handling and one retry on malformed JSON.

code_generation.adjustments: Parameterize model via OPENAI_MODEL, pass temperature to Responses API, add robust response extraction, and implement strict LLM requirement with OFFLINE_MODE override. Replace heuristic StarRater with LLM JSON output. Fix item prior smoothing (blend with global mean) and center biases on global_mean. Add a small cache for generations. Add aspect aliasing/lemmatization and profanity checks.

code_verification.adjustments: Add smoke tests: verify all five files are loaded; check OPENAI_API_KEY presence triggers strict mode; confirm LLM star rater returns an int in [1,5]; validate priors smoothing uses global_mean; ensure temperature/model are passed to the API.

simulation_execution.adjustments: Enable caching to reduce duplicate LLM calls, limit per-trial generations for tuning, log model name/temperature/latency, and ensure deterministic sampling where applicable.

result_evaluation.adjustments: Even in ODD mode, implement optional hooks to compute MAE/RMSE on test splits and a semantic similarity metric (fallback to token-based if embeddings not available). Track Aspect_Coverage and policy penalties.

feedback_generation.adjustments: Provide clear error messages for missing files or API key, and actionable warnings when policy violations or aspect coverage gaps are detected. Summarize LLM configuration in the run report for reproducibility.

human_feedback:
In the code: 
Please read the correct data files as specified in the task requirements:
amazon_train_sample.json (training data), amazon_test_sample.json (testing data), user_sample.json (user information), item_sample.json (item information), and review_sample.json (historical reviews).

Use user details from user_sample.json, item metadata from item_sample.json, and historical reviews from review_sample.json as the data sources for reasoning.

Please use LLM to reason the Star Rating, don't use heuristic rules.
